title: Fresh Strawberry Cupcakes with White Chocolate Frosting
ingredients: 8 ounces white chocolate, chopped (do not use chips)
3 packages (8 ounces each) cream cheese, room temperature
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F and line a cupcake pan with liners.
Cream butter and sugar on medium speed for 3–5 minutes, until fluffy.
Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Combine flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add dry ingredients to creamed mixture alternately with milk, mixing until smooth.
Fill cupcake liners ½ to ¾ full.
Bake 20–25 minutes, until a toothpick inserted comes out clean.
Melt white chocolate over a double boiler just until smooth and warm, being careful not to overheat, then remove from heat.
Beat in 1¼ cups powdered sugar, then warm white chocolate.
Whip chilled heavy whipping cream separately until beginning to thicken.
Add remaining powdered sugar to whipped cream and beat to medium-firm peaks.
Fold whipped cream into cream cheese mixture in three additions.
Cover frosting and refrigerate several hours before using.
notes: Do not use white chocolate chips for the frosting; use chopped white chocolate instead
Frosting should be refrigerated several hours before using
This recipe was served at Katie and Patrick's wedding reception
